name: FancyTable

unions.name: FancyTable
unions.comment: The base model of the table

unions.name: Msg
unions.comment: The basic type accepted by the update

aliases.name: Header
aliases.comment:
Represents the content that goes into your THead
# index = order indes
# name = the string name for the column
# content = html inside the header
# settings = settings for this header
aliases.type: { index : Int, name : String, content : Html Msg, settings : HeaderSettings }

aliases.name: HeaderSettings
aliases.comment: Represents the settings for the Header
aliases.type: { minWidth : Int, maxWidth : Int, width : Int, isVisible : Bool }

aliases.name: Settings
aliases.comment: Represents the table settings to be able to resize/reorder columns
aliases.type: { resizeColumns : Bool, reorderColumns : Bool, hideColumns : Bool }

values.name: getTableRowFromHtml
values.comment: get table row from a list of Html Msg (content)

values.name: getTableRowFromStrings
values.comment: get table row from a list of strings (content)

values.name: init
values.comment: Returns default fancy table which is an empty table

values.name: setHeaderMaxWidth
values.comment: Sets the max width of the header when rendered.

values.name: setHeaderMinWidth
values.comment: Sets the min width of the header when rendered.

values.name: setHeaderWidth
values.comment: Sets the width of the header when rendered.

values.name: setTableHeaders
values.comment: Sets the talbe headers from a list of Header

values.name: setTableHeadersFromHtml
values.comment:
Sets the table headers from a list of (String, Html Msg) (content)
where the String is the header name and the Html Msg is the header content
values.type: HeaderSettings -> List ( String, Html Msg ) -> FancyTable -> FancyTable

values.name: setTableHeadersFromStrings
values.comment: Sets the table headers from a list of strings (content)

values.name: setTableRows
values.comment: Sets the table rows from a list of TableData

values.name: subscriptions
values.comment: Returns the subscriptions necessary to run

values.name: update
values.comment: takes a model and a message and applies it to create an updated model
